title: How to Design Deep Games
creator: Jonathan Blow
description: A keynote about pursuing difficult, open-ended creative work, developing specialized judgment, and sustaining projects whose destination cannot be fully specified before the work begins.
useful: It addresses the human process behind ambitious independent games and offers a counterweight to production advice built only around speed, predictability, and market validation.

manualLinkStatus.note: Conference recording opened successfully during editorial review.

audience: Independent developers and small teams deciding whether a difficult long-term project deserves the time, uncertainty, and specialization it demands.
learning: Distinguish deep exploration from routine production work
Use internal direction when a project cannot be fully planned
Assess the personal cost and value of long creative projects

editorial.note: Verified against the published conference recording.
